translated: ProSeka AfterTalk for... Reaching Out... yeah!
Good evening, everyone.
I'm Hinata Sato, the voice of Mizuki Akiyama from Nightcord at 25:00.
This ProSeka AfterTalk is a stream where we look back on the event while reading your letters.
This time, we're covering the "Reaching Out for a Tangled Tomorrow" event.
I hope you all enjoyed the story.
The event just ended yesterday, so how was it for everyone?
How are you all doing?
I hope you had a great time with it.
I think it was a story about the members of Niigo facing their futures...
...and thinking about what they want to do.
It probably really hit home for those of you who are at the age where you have to decide on your career path.
We've updated the AfterTalk format since April, and we've been collecting letters via the official ProSeka Discord and email.
Thank you so much to everyone who sent one in.
I'll be reading your letters during the show, so please look forward to it.
If you want to tweet your thoughts on the event or this stream, please use the hashtag #ProSekaAfterTalk.
I'm keeping an eye on the YouTube chat and I'll check the hashtag later too.
I'll try to pick out as many interesting comments as I can.
It's almost all comments from overseas! I can't even find the Japanese ones. People are saying my ribbon is cute, or that my hair matches Mizuki's.
The stream is probably lagging a bit right now, so people are just now mentioning my hair and outfit.
I dressed up with the illustration in mind today... well, I always do, but especially today.
I really tried to match the art.
Good evening!
Good evening, everyone! Thank you for joining.
Oh, someone said, "The story and the new song were so good, I cried my eyes out!"
I'll talk about the song in a bit.
I was at home like, "What on earth was that?!"
I was just stunned by it.
I really felt the developers' dedication to their craft.
Yes, exactly.
I'll talk more about that later.
Now, let's dive right into the story for "Reaching Out for a Tangled Tomorrow".
We've received letters about this story as well...
...so I'd like to look back on it while reading them.
First, we have a letter from Kotori no Mama-san.
They wrote, "Hi, Hina-san! Konnigo!"
Konnigo!
"My child and I both enjoy playing ProSeka together."
"The event featuring my favorite, Mizuki-chan, was so cute, funny, and moving."
"I'm glad I could see all the different feelings Mizuki has through their experiences and relationships."
"Staying true to yourself and not giving up on what you love can be hard when you hit a wall."
"But I believe that if you hold onto those feelings, you'll be rewarded someday."
"I believe there are definitely people out there, like the members of Niigo..."
"...who will accept and recognize you for who you are."
"I really love the stories in ProSeka."
"Hina-san, thank you so much for always voicing them."
"I'll continue to support both Mizuki-chan and Hina-san with all my heart!"
Thank you so much, Kotori no Mama-san!
In this story, her sister coming home from overseas for the pop-up shop was the start, and Mizuki wanting the Niigo members to meet her felt like a huge step forward.
Kanade and Mafuyu had met Yuuki-san before, but Ena...
...met her for the first time this time. The story really moved forward in various ways.
Mizuki loves making videos, but they also have so many other passions...
...like remaking clothes, visiting boutiques, and looking at makeup.
I think it's amazing that they're already facing the question of what they want to aim for in the future.
When I was looking at colleges, I felt like if I was going to pay for it, I had to study something that would give me a trade, so I just visited open campuses constantly.
So I felt like my approach to finding what I like was completely different from Mizuki's.
Even so, I think it was a really important time for Mizuki to re-examine what they love at their own pace.
Realizing that a passion isn't just a hobby, but something you might want to turn into a career, is a huge deal, so I'm just glad Mizuki was able to smile.
